source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A closed, square loop is formed with 40 cm of wire having \( R = 0.10 \, \Omega \), as shown in figure. A 0.50 T magnetic field is perpendicular to the loop. At \( t = 0 \, \text{s} \), two diagonally opposite corners of the loop begin to move apart. How long does it take the loop to collapse to a straight line?

image_dependency: question explicitly says 'as shown in figure'
the setup involves geometric/positional change of diagonally opposite corners, which is likely specified by the diagram
asset registry confirms an image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: question asks for a time to collapse under a changing loop geometry, suggesting more than direct lookup
problem combines physical quantities from text (wire length, resistance, magnetic field) with the changing shape described in the figure
multiple-choice numeric physics question likely requires deriving or combining intermediate relations before selecting an answer
conservative scoring because the exact figure details and intended solution path are not fully recoverable from text alone
verifiability: answer is a single numeric value: 0.1 s
choices are provided, enabling straightforward final-answer checking
asset registry marks answer_present=true and registry_passed=true
physics solution steps would likely be checkable from explicit givens, though some dependence on the unseen figure remains
